summary: Preflight endpoint for estimating how many award, transaction, or subaward rows a prospective advanced-search download would contain and whether that result exceeds current row-limited download caps.
businessPurpose: Use this endpoint before starting a row-limited download to estimate result size, choose the correct spending grain, and decide whether you must tighten filters or switch to larger-volume download workflows.
analyticalGrain: One aggregate preflight result per request body, representing the count of records at the resolved spending_level plus legacy alias fields, limit flags, and advisory messages.

primaryEntities.name: Filtered download population
primaryEntities.description: The set of award, transaction, or subaward rows that would qualify for a related custom download under the supplied advanced filters.

primaryEntities.name: Spending level
primaryEntities.description: The business grain requested by the caller: transactions by default, or awards or subawards when specified.

measures.name: calculated_count
measures.description: Primary count for the resolved spending_level. This is the main measure to use when deciding whether a row-limited download is feasible.

measures.name: maximum_limit and rows_gt_limit
measures.description: Together these tell you whether the requested count fits inside the current row-limited custom download cap.

measures.name: Legacy transaction_* aliases
measures.description: calculated_transaction_count and its companion limit fields currently mirror the primary measure instead of providing a distinct second measure.

dimensions.name: Advanced filters
dimensions.description: The filters object supports keyword, time, agency, recipient, geography, award-type, account, and industry constraints shared with USAspending advanced search and download experiences.

dimensions.name: Requested grain
dimensions.description: spending_level changes the meaning of calculated_count from transactions to awards or subawards.

dimensions.name: Time-period interpretation
dimensions.description: time_period.date_type can change which date dimension is used for filtering, and date_signed is live for this endpoint.

suitableQuestions.name: Will this filtered download exceed the custom download row cap?
suitableQuestions.description: Use rows_gt_limit and maximum_limit before launching a row-limited download job.

suitableQuestions.name: How many award-level versus transaction-level rows match the same filter set?
suitableQuestions.description: Call the endpoint with the same filters and compare calculated_count across spending_level values before choosing a downstream export workflow.

suitableQuestions.name: How many transaction rows match a specific date interpretation such as date_signed?
suitableQuestions.description: Use time_period.date_type when the date basis of the filter matters for the preflight estimate.

notSuitableFor.name: Returning record details
notSuitableFor.description: This endpoint never returns award, transaction, or subaward rows; it only returns aggregate counts and messages.

notSuitableFor.name: Producing grouped analytics
notSuitableFor.description: It does not break counts out by agency, geography, recipient, or time bucket. Use search aggregation endpoints for grouped analysis.

notSuitableFor.name: Actually starting the download job
notSuitableFor.description: Count responses are preflight metadata only. Separate download endpoints create files or jobs.

notSuitableFor.name: Searching pre-FY2008 time slices through search-style time_period filters
notSuitableFor.description: The search filter docs and live messages warn that search start and end dates are limited to an earliest date of 2007-10-01 for this workflow.

on: Reuse the same filters when rows_gt_limit is false and you need an award-oriented custom download job.
joins.description: Use Download Count to preflight a custom award download before calling the asynchronous award download endpoint.

on: Switch workflows when the estimated count exceeds maximum_limit or when you need older data outside the search time-period window.
joins.description: Counts that exceed the row-limited cap, or time ranges earlier than 2007-10-01, point toward bulk or website-based larger-volume workflows instead of row-limited custom downloads.

workflows.name: Preflight a custom download
workflows.description: Estimate size and cap risk before creating a row-limited download job.

steps.action: POST filters and optional spending_level to v2__download__count to retrieve calculated_count, maximum_limit, rows_gt_limit, and advisory messages.
If rows_gt_limit is false, reuse the filters in the appropriate download endpoint; otherwise narrow filters or switch to a bulk workflow.

caveats.statement: Treat calculated_count plus spending_level as the authoritative business measure. The legacy transaction_* fields currently behave as aliases and are explicitly marked for future deprecation in live messages.
caveats.impact: Do not interpret calculated_transaction_count as a second independent metric when spending_level is awards or subawards.

caveats.statement: A 200 response can still mean some supplied filter keys were ignored.
caveats.impact: Inspect messages for unused-filter warnings when debugging unexpectedly broad counts.

caveats.statement: Many less-common filter branches are preserved from documentation and source but were not re-verified live in this bundle.
caveats.impact: Use them as supported request shapes with some residual uncertainty, rather than assuming the MCP surface is limited to the probed filters.
